codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all 7 feature requirements. ReportPage.tsx enhanced with screenshot timeline (chronological screenshots from findings and events, expandable on click), PDF download button, Compare Personas navigation, Back to History link, failed audit error state, and loading skeleton. API client updated with getPdfUrl(). 23 comprehensive tests added. Code is well-structured with clear helper functions for screenshot extraction and deduplication. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Expanded image modal uses extensive inline styles (~30 lines) instead of CSS classes in src/styles/index.css. Project conventions (AGENTS.md, SKILL.md) specify 'Plain CSS in src/styles/index.css — no CSS-in-JS'. While inline styles technically work, they break the established pattern and make theming harder. Should use CSS classes like .screenshot-modal, .screenshot-modal-overlay, .screenshot-modal-close.

extractScreenshotsFromFindings() and extractScreenshotsFromEvents() both sort their results internally, and then screenshotTimeline useMemo (line 204) sorts the combined array again. The intermediate sorts are redundant since the final sort produces the correct ordering regardless.

parseStepFromUrl() assumes URL filenames follow '{scenario}_{persona}_{step}.png' format. This is fragile — actual screenshot URLs may not follow this convention. However, the 'unknown' fallback mitigates runtime errors. In practice, the scenario/persona from this function are unused since extractScreenshotsFromFindings takes those from the finding object directly.

Modal accessibility: while tabIndex={0} and Escape key handler are present, the modal does not programmatically receive focus when opened. Screen reader users and keyboard-only users may not be aware the modal opened. Consider adding a useEffect with ref.focus() when expandedImage changes.

Loading skeleton is minimal — a static 'Loading...' pill and 'Loading report data...' text rather than a true skeleton UI with shimmering placeholder shapes. Functionally correct but the description said 'loading skeleton' which typically implies animated placeholder shapes.

Several test mock objects use partial evidence_payload (e.g., { screenshot_urls: [] }) that don't match the full Finding type interface. TypeScript allows this in tests due to the overrides spread pattern, but it could mask type issues if the Finding interface changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used extensive inline styles for the screenshot modal overlay, image, and close button (~30 lines of inline style objects) instead of CSS classes in src/styles/index.css. AGENTS.md says 'Plain CSS in src/styles/index.css — no Tailwind, no CSS-in-JS, no CSS modules' and SKILL.md says 'CSS goes in src/styles/index.css'. Inline React style objects are technically CSS-in-JS. This convention could be made more explicit: 'Do not use inline style objects for layout/theming; use CSS classes in index.css.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/pages/ReportPage.tsx lines 475-540 — modal overlay, image, and close button all use style={{...}} objects with positioning, colors, and layout properties that should be in index.css.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker only ran the ReportPage-specific test (npx vitest run src/pages/ReportPage.test.tsx) rather than the full frontend test suite (npm test) during verification. The handoff shows only one verification command. The SKILL.md procedure says to run 'cd frontend && npm test' plus lint, typecheck, and build.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun shows only: 'cd C:\EthicalSiteInspector\frontend && npx vitest run src/pages/ReportPage.test.tsx'. Missing: npm run lint, npx tsc -b, npm run build.

summary: Feature passes review. All 7 requirements implemented: screenshot timeline with expandable thumbnails, PDF download button, Compare Personas navigation, Back to History link, failed audit error state, loading state, and getPdfUrl() in API client. 23 tests cover all major features including loading, error, screenshot expansion/close, navigation links, and findings grouping. Non-blocking issues: inline styles in modal (should use CSS classes per conventions), redundant intermediate sorting, minimal loading skeleton, and incomplete verification command coverage. No blocking issues.
